message:
chore(beads): close bd-3u5d3.6 (session type obligation tests)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -2528,7 +2528,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-3u5d3.3","title":"[ALIEN-GRAVEYARD] Convert SendPermit/Ack obligation to session types","description":"Convert the SendPermit->Ack protocol to use session types. This is the simplest obligation protocol (binary, no branching) and serves as the proof-of-concept for the session type system.

BACKGROUND (Session Types, Graveyard section 5.2): Session types encode communication protocols in the type system. A session-typed channel Channel<S> can only perform the operation specified by state S. After performing that operation, the channel transitions to the next state. Protocol violations become compile errors. Key paper: Honda 1993 (binary session types).

CURRENT STATE: SendPermit and Ack are runtime-checked tokens. A task acquires a SendPermit, sends a message, and receives an Ack. Protocol violations (e.g., sending without a permit, acking without receiving) are caught at runtime via panics or Result errors.

TARGET STATE: Channel<SendState> can only call send() which returns Channel<AckState>, which can only call ack() which returns Channel<Done>. Attempting to call ack() before send() is a compile error. The session-typed API wraps the existing runtime-checked API so both can coexist during migration.

RUST IMPLEMENTATION GUIDANCE: (1) Define state types: struct SendReady; struct WaitingAck; struct Done;. (2) Define typed channel: struct ObligationChannel<S> { inner: RawChannel, _state: PhantomData<S> }. (3) Implement transitions: impl ObligationChannel<SendReady> { fn send(self, msg: M) -> ObligationChannel<WaitingAck> { ... } } -- note self is consumed (moved), preventing reuse. (4) Use the typestate macros from bd-3u5d3.2 to generate these types from a protocol spec rather than hand-coding. (5) Integrate with Cx: the send() method requires Cx<SendCapability> proving the caller has permission. (6) Backward compat: impl From<ObligationChannel<SendReady>> for LegacySendPermit for gradual migration.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: (1) Calling send() on a Channel<SendReady> compiles and returns Channel<WaitingAck>. (2) Calling ack() on a Channel<SendReady> fails to compile with a clear error message. (3) Calling send() twice on the same channel fails to compile (moved value). (4) Zero runtime overhead vs current implementation (verified by criterion.rs benchmark). (5) Legacy API still works and interoperates with session-typed API. (6) All protocol transitions logged to EvidenceLedger.

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS: Each state transition emits a structured log via tracing: obligation_channel_id, from_state, to_state, transition (send|ack), trace_id, task_id. EvidenceLedger entry on channel creation and completion: component=obligation_channel, action=send_permit_ack_complete, includes total_transition_count and latency_us. On protocol error (legacy API path): ERROR log with channel_id, expected_state, actual_state.

TESTING APPROACH: (1) Use trybuild crate for compile-fail tests verifying invalid protocol sequences produce errors. (2) Unit tests for happy path: create channel, send, ack, verify Done state. (3) Integration test: run SendPermit/Ack protocol under FrankenLab with 100 concurrent channels. (4) Backward compat test: legacy API and session-typed API produce identical observable behavior. (5) Benchmark: criterion.rs comparing session-typed vs legacy performance.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:15:41.606839665Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T20:33:36.786094324Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T20:33:36.785965435Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-graveyard","session-types"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3u5d3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1usdh.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:26Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3u5d3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3u5d3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:26Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3u5d3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3u5d3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:26Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3u5d3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3u5d3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:26Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3u5d3.4","title":"[ALIEN-GRAVEYARD] Convert Lease/Release obligation to session types","description":"Convert the Lease->Release protocol to session types. This is more complex than SendPermit because leases have timeout semantics, creating a branching protocol.

BACKGROUND (Session Types with Choice, Graveyard section 5.2): Binary session types support choice (branching) via offer/select. When a protocol has multiple possible continuations, the type system tracks which branch was taken. This maps naturally to lease protocols where a lease can be released or can expire.

CURRENT STATE: Lease and Release are runtime-checked. A task acquires a lease, holds it for some duration, then either releases it explicitly or lets it expire. Timeout detection is handled by a separate watchdog.

TARGET STATE: Channel<LeaseActive> offers two transitions: (1) release() -> Channel<Done> for explicit release, (2) The timeout watcher can force-transition to Channel<Expired> via an external event. This tests the choice/branching encoding in the typestate system.

RUST IMPLEMENTATION GUIDANCE: (1) Define state types: struct LeaseActive; struct LeaseExpired; struct Done;. (2) Choice encoding: use an enum for the branch outcome: enum LeaseOutcome { Released(Channel<Done>), Expired(Channel<LeaseExpired>) }. (3) Timeout integration: impl ObligationChannel<LeaseActive> { async fn hold_or_expire(self, deadline: Instant) -> LeaseOutcome } uses tokio::select! internally. (4) Renewal as recursion: impl ObligationChannel<LeaseActive> { fn renew(self, new_deadline: Instant) -> ObligationChannel<LeaseActive> } consumes and re-creates the channel in the same state. (5) Cx deadline propagation: the lease deadline is derived from Cx::deadline(), ensuring attenuated capabilities cannot extend leases beyond their parent budget. (6) Use typestate macros from bd-3u5d3.2 to generate from protocol spec.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: (1) Calling release() on Channel<LeaseActive> compiles and returns Channel<Done>. (2) Accessing the channel after expiry produces a compile error (moved value). (3) Renewal resets the deadline without releasing the lease (same logical channel, new timeout). (4) Lease duration respects Cx deadline propagation. (5) Zero runtime overhead vs current implementation. (6) All transitions logged to EvidenceLedger.

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS: Each lease lifecycle event emits structured log: lease_id, event_type (acquired|renewed|released|expired), remaining_budget_ms, trace_id. EvidenceLedger entry on lease completion: component=lease_manager, action=lease_released|lease_expired, includes hold_duration_ms, renewal_count, was_within_budget.

TESTING APPROACH: (1) trybuild compile-fail tests for invalid lease operations. (2) Unit tests: acquire, hold, release -- verify Done state. (3) Timeout test: acquire lease with short deadline, verify expiry path fires correctly. (4) Renewal test: acquire, renew 5 times, release -- verify all transitions. (5) Virtual time test via FrankenLab: verify lease expiry is deterministic under virtual clock. (6) Concurrent leases: 100 leases with staggered deadlines, verify no cross-contamination.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:15:47.219547948Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T20:35:50.856640086Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T20:35:50.856506067Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-graveyard","session-types"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3u5d3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1usdh.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:30Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3u5d3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3u5d3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:30Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3u5d3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3u5d3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:30Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3u5d3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3u5d3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:30Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3u5d3.5","title":"[ALIEN-GRAVEYARD] Convert Reserve/Commit two-phase effect to session types","description":"Convert the Reserve->Commit two-phase effect protocol to session types. This is the most complex obligation protocol because it involves branching (commit vs abort) and potential delegation (passing reserved effects to child tasks).

BACKGROUND (Session Types with Delegation, Graveyard section 5.2): Session type delegation allows passing a typed channel to another participant, transferring protocol responsibility. This maps to two-phase commit where a coordinator reserves effects, then delegates the commit decision to a child task. Honda-Yoshida-Carbone 2008 extends this to multiparty settings.

CURRENT STATE: Reserve and Commit are runtime-checked tokens. A task reserves an effect (tentatively allocating resources), then either commits (making it permanent) or aborts (rolling back). The reserved effect can be passed to child tasks for delegation.

TARGET STATE: Channel<Reserved<E>> offers three transitions: (1) commit() -> Channel<Committed<E>> making the effect permanent, (2) abort() -> Channel<Aborted> rolling back, (3) delegate(child_task) -> passes the Channel<Reserved<E>> to a child (session delegation). Nested two-phase effects compose: a Channel<Reserved<(E1, E2)>> can partially commit E1 while keeping E2 reserved.

RUST IMPLEMENTATION GUIDANCE: (1) State types: struct Reserved<E: Effect>; struct Committed<E: Effect>; struct Aborted;. (2) Choice encoding: enum TwoPhaseOutcome<E: Effect> { Committed(Channel<Committed<E>>), Aborted(Channel<Aborted>) }. (3) Delegation: impl ObligationChannel<Reserved<E>> { fn delegate(self) -> DelegationHandle<E> } where DelegationHandle can be sent to a child task via the capability context. The child receives ObligationChannel<Reserved<E>> and must commit or abort -- the type system ensures the child cannot ignore the obligation. (4) Nested composition: implement via tuple effects Reserved<(E1, E2)> with partial commit: fn commit_first(self) -> ObligationChannel<Reserved<E2>>. (5) Compensation: abort generates a compensation action that is the inverse of the reserved effect -- use a Compensate<E> associated type on the Effect trait.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: (1) commit() and abort() are mutually exclusive at compile time (self consumed). (2) Delegated channels must be committed or aborted by the delegate -- dropping without resolution is a compile error (must_use). (3) Nested effects can be partially committed. (4) Compensation actions fire on abort. (5) Zero overhead vs runtime-checked implementation. (6) Full delegation chain logged to EvidenceLedger.

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS: Each two-phase lifecycle event emits: effect_id, event_type (reserved|committed|aborted|delegated), delegated_to (task_id), nesting_depth, trace_id. EvidenceLedger entry on completion: component=two_phase_effect, action=committed|aborted, includes hold_duration_ms, delegation_chain_length, compensation_triggered.

TESTING APPROACH: (1) trybuild compile-fail tests: cannot use channel after commit, cannot commit and abort same channel. (2) Unit tests: reserve-commit, reserve-abort, reserve-delegate-commit, reserve-delegate-abort. (3) Nested tests: reserve (E1,E2), commit E1, abort E2 -- verify partial commit semantics. (4) Property tests (proptest): generate random sequences of reserve/commit/abort/delegate and verify all paths are valid or correctly rejected. (5) FrankenLab integration: 50 concurrent two-phase effects with random delegation, verify zero leaks.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:15:52.924465445Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T20:42:38.219667110Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T20:42:38.219533232Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-graveyard","session-types","two-phase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3u5d3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1usdh.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:20Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3u5d3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3u5d3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:20Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3u5d3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3u5d3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:20Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3u5d3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3u5d3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:20Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3u5d3.6","title":"[ALIEN-GRAVEYARD] Unit tests for session type obligation protocols","description":"Comprehensive test suite for session-typed obligations. UNIT TESTS: (1) Happy path for each converted protocol (SendPermit/Ack, Lease/Release, Reserve/Commit) -- verify correct state transitions and final Done state. (2) Compile-time rejection of invalid protocol sequences (trybuild compile-fail tests): (a) calling ack() before send(), (b) calling send() twice on moved channel, (c) using channel after commit, (d) dropping Reserved channel without commit/abort. (3) Protocol composition (nesting) -- nested two-phase within a lease. (4) Protocol delegation -- passing typed channel to child task. (5) Integration with Cx capability contexts -- transitions require correct capabilities. (6) Backward compatibility -- legacy runtime-checked API interoperates with session-typed API. PROPERTY-BASED TESTS (proptest): (7) For randomly generated valid protocol sequences, all execute without error. (8) For randomly generated invalid sequences, all are rejected at compile time (via trybuild) or runtime (via Result::Err). (9) Session type overhead is zero: benchmark session-typed vs legacy for 10000 protocol executions, assert delta < 1%. E2E TESTS: (10) Run 100 concurrent SendPermit/Ack sessions under FrankenLab, verify zero leaks. (11) Run 50 concurrent Lease/Release sessions with staggered deadlines and cancellations. (12) Run 30 concurrent Reserve/Commit sessions with random delegation chains. LOGGING REQUIREMENTS: Each test logs: test_name, protocol_name, assertion_count, compile_fail_tests_run, pass_fail, execution_time_ms. On failure: log full protocol state trace showing each transition. CI integration: trybuild tests pinned to specific Rust compiler version for stable error messages. ACCEPTANCE: All compile-fail tests produce clear error messages. Zero overhead vs runtime-checked API. Zero leaks across all concurrent E2E scenarios. All proptest properties hold for 10000 random inputs.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:15:58.962700203Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T02:09:01.275578969Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-graveyard","session-types","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3u5d3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1hu19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:47Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3u5d3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3u5d3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:47Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3u5d3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3u5d3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:47Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3u5d3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3u5d3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:47Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3u5d3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3u5d3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:47Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3u5d3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3u5d3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:47Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3u7","title":"[Integration] Wire All RaptorQ Modules Together","description":"# asupersync-3u7: Wire All RaptorQ Modules Together

## Bead Type: Integration

---

## Overview and Purpose

The `asupersync-3u7` bead provides the integration layer that connects all RaptorQ-related modules into a cohesive, ergonomic API. This bead is the "glue" that makes the RaptorQ distributed layer usable as a unified system rather than a collection of independent components.

### Goals

1. **Unified Configuration**: Single configuration facade that propagates settings to all subsystems (encoding, decoding, transport, security, observability)
2. **Builder Patterns**: Ergonomic builders for constructing end-to-end pipelines with sensible defaults
3. **End-to-End Pipelines**: Pre-composed pipelines for common use cases (reliable broadcast, point-to-point, store-and-forward)
4. **Integration Tests**: Comprehensive tests validating cross-module interactions

### Non-Goals

- Implementing new encoding/decoding algorithms (handled by dedicated beads)
- Network I/O implementation (uses traits from transport layer)
- Cryptographic primitives (uses `security` module)

---

## Core Types

### Configuration Facade

```rust
//! Unified configuration for the RaptorQ distributed layer.

use crate::observability::ObservabilityConfig;
use crate::security::{AuthKey, AuthMode};
use crate::types::{Budget, Time};
use std::time::Duration;

/// Master configuration for the RaptorQ integration layer.
///
/// This facade aggregates configuration for all subsystems and provides
/// sensible defaults while allowing fine-grained customization.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct RaptorQConfig {
    /// Encoding parameters.
    pub encoding: EncodingConfig,
    /// Decoding parameters.
    pub decoding: DecodingConfig,
    /// Transport parameters.
    pub transport: TransportConfig,
    /// Security configuration.
    pub security: SecurityConfig,
    /// Observability settings.
    pub observability: ObservabilityConfig,
    /// Resource budgets.
    pub budgets: BudgetConfig,
}

/// Encoding-specific configuration.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct EncodingConfig {
    /// Symbol size in bytes (default: 1280 per RFC 6330).
    pub symbol_size: u16,
    /// Maximum source symbols per block.
    pub max_symbols_per_block: u16,
    /// Repair symbol overhead ratio (e.g., 0.1 = 10% extra symbols).
    pub repair_overhead: f64,
    /// Whether to interleave symbols across blocks.
    pub interleave: bool,
    /// Maximum concurrent encoding operations.
    pub max_concurrent_encodings: usize,
}

/// Decoding-specific configuration.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct DecodingConfig {
    /// Minimum symbols required before attempting decode (ratio of K).
    pub decode_threshold: f64,
    /// Maximum time to wait for sufficient symbols.
    pub decode_timeout: Duration,
    /// Whether to attempt progressive decoding.
    pub progressive: bool,
    /// Maximum memory for pending symbols per object.
    pub max_pending_bytes: usize,
    /// Maximum concurrent decoding operations.
    pub max_concurrent_decodings: usize,
}

/// Transport-specific configuration.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct TransportConfig {
    /// Maximum symbols in flight (not yet acknowledged).
    pub max_in_flight: usize,
    /// Send rate limit (symbols per second, 0 = unlimited).
    pub rate_limit: u32,
    /// Retry policy for failed transmissions.
    pub retry_policy: RetryPolicy,
    /// Connection pool size per peer.
    pub pool_size: usize,
    /// Idle connection timeout.
    pub idle_timeout: Duration,
}

/// Retry policy configuration.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct RetryPolicy {
    /// Maximum retry attempts.
    pub max_attempts: u8,
    /// Initial backoff delay.
    pub initial_delay: Duration,
    /// Maximum backoff delay.
    pub max_delay: Duration,
    /// Backoff multiplier.
    pub multiplier: f64,
}

/// Security configuration.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct SecurityConfig {
    /// Authentication mode.
    pub auth_mode: AuthMode,
    /// Key material (if auth is enabled).
    pub auth_key: Option<AuthKey>,
    /// Whether to reject unauthenticated symbols.
    pub reject_unauthenticated: bool,
}

/// Budget configuration.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct BudgetConfig {
    /// Default budget for encoding operations.
    pub encode_budget: Budget,
    /// Default budget for decoding operations.
    pub decode_budget: Budget,
    /// Default budget for transport operations.
    pub transport_budget: Budget,
}

impl Default for RaptorQConfig {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            encoding: EncodingConfig::default(),
            decoding: DecodingConfig::default(),
            transport: TransportConfig::default(),
            security: SecurityConfig::default(),
            observability: ObservabilityConfig::default(),
            budgets: BudgetConfig::default(),
        }
    }
}

impl Default for EncodingConfig {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            symbol_size: 1280,
            max_symbols_per_block: 256,
            repair_overhead: 0.1,
            interleave: true,
            max_concurrent_encodings: 4,
        }
    }
}

impl Default for DecodingConfig {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            decode_threshold: 1.0,
            decode_timeout: Duration::from_secs(30),
            progressive: true,
            max_pending_bytes: 64 * 1024 * 1024, // 64 MB
            max_concurrent_decodings: 4,
        }
    }
}

impl Default for TransportConfig {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            max_in_flight: 100,
            rate_limit: 0,
            retry_policy: RetryPolicy::default(),
            pool_size: 4,
            idle_timeout: Duration::from_secs(60),
        }
    }
}

impl Default for RetryPolicy {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            max_attempts: 3,
            initial_delay: Duration::from_millis(100),
            max_delay: Duration::from_secs(10),
            multiplier: 2.0,
        }
    }
}

impl Default for SecurityConfig {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            auth_mode: AuthMode::Optional,
            auth_key: None,
            reject_unauthenticated: false,
        }
    }
}

impl Default for BudgetConfig {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            encode_budget: Budget::new().with_poll_quota(10_000),
            decode_budget: Budget::new().with_poll_quota(50_000),
            transport_budget: Budget::new().with_poll_quota(5_000),
        }
    }
}
```

### Builder Patterns

```rust
//! Builders for constructing RaptorQ pipelines.

use crate::types::symbol::{ObjectId, Symbol};
use crate::error::Result;

/// Builder for constructing a RaptorQ sender pipeline.
///
/// # Example
///
/// ```ignore
/// let sender = RaptorQSenderBuilder::new()
///     .with_config(config)
///     .with_transport(transport)
///     .with_security_context(security_ctx)
///     .with_metrics(metrics)
///     .build()?;
///
/// sender.send_object(object_id, data).await?;
/// ```
#[derive(Default)]
pub struct RaptorQSenderBuilder<T = ()> {
    config: Option<RaptorQConfig>,
    transport: Option<T>,
    security_context: Option<SecurityContext>,
    metrics: Option<Metrics>,
    diagnostic_context: Option<DiagnosticContext>,
}

impl<T> RaptorQSenderBuilder<T> {
    /// Creates a new builder with defaults.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        Self {
            config: None,
            transport: None,
            security_context: None,
            metrics: None,
            diagnostic_context: None,
        }
    }

    /// Sets the configuration.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn with_config(mut self, config: RaptorQConfig) -> Self {
        self.config = Some(config);
        self
    }

    /// Sets the transport implementation.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn with_transport<U>(self, transport: U) -> RaptorQSenderBuilder<U> {
        RaptorQSenderBuilder {
            config: self.config,
            transport: Some(transport),
            security_context: self.security_context,
            metrics: self.metrics,
            diagnostic_context: self.diagnostic_context,
        }
    }

    /// Sets the security context.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn with_security_context(mut self, ctx: SecurityContext) -> Self {
        self.security_context = Some(ctx);
        self
    }

    /// Sets the metrics registry.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn with_metrics(mut self, metrics: Metrics) -> Self {
        self.metrics = Some(metrics);
        self
    }

    /// Sets the diagnostic context for tracing.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn with_diagnostic_context(mut self, ctx: DiagnosticContext) -> Self {
        self.diagnostic_context = Some(ctx);
        self
    }
}

impl<T: SymbolSink> RaptorQSenderBuilder<T> {
    /// Builds the sender pipeline.
    ///
    /// # Errors
    ///
    /// Returns an error if required components are missing or configuration is invalid.
    pub fn build(self) -> Result<RaptorQSender<T>> {
        let config = self.config.unwrap_or_default();
        let transport = self.transport.ok_or_else(|| {
            Error::new(ErrorKind::InvalidEncodingParams)
                .with_context("transport is required")
        })?;

        Ok(RaptorQSender {
            config,
            transport,
            security_context: self.security_context,
            metrics: self.metrics,
            diagnostic_context: self.diagnostic_context,
        })
    }
}

/// Builder for constructing a RaptorQ receiver pipeline.
#[derive(Default)]
pub struct RaptorQReceiverBuilder<S = ()> {
    config: Option<RaptorQConfig>,
    source: Option<S>,
    security_context: Option<SecurityContext>,
    metrics: Option<Metrics>,
    diagnostic_context: Option<DiagnosticContext>,
}

impl<S> RaptorQReceiverBuilder<S> {
    /// Creates a new builder with defaults.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        Self {
            config: None,
            source: None,
            security_context: None,
            metrics: None,
            diagnostic_context: None,
        }
    }

    /// Sets the configuration.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn with_config(mut self, config: RaptorQConfig) -> Self {
        self.config = Some(config);
        self
    }

    /// Sets the symbol source.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn with_source<U>(self, source: U) -> RaptorQReceiverBuilder<U> {
        RaptorQReceiverBuilder {
            config: self.config,
            source: Some(source),
            security_context: self.security_context,
            metrics: self.metrics,
            diagnostic_context: self.diagnostic_context,
        }
    }

    /// Sets the security context.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn with_security_context(mut self, ctx: SecurityContext) -> Self {
        self.security_context = Some(ctx);
        self
    }

    /// Sets the metrics registry.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn with_metrics(mut self, metrics: Metrics) -> Self {
        self.metrics = Some(metrics);
        self
    }

    /// Sets the diagnostic context for tracing.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn with_diagnostic_context(mut self, ctx: DiagnosticContext) -> Self {
        self.diagnostic_context = Some(ctx);
        self
    }
}

impl<S: SymbolSource> RaptorQReceiverBuilder<S> {
    /// Builds the receiver pipeline.
    ///
    /// # Errors
    ///
    /// Returns an error if required components are missing or configuration is invalid.
    pub fn build(self) -> Result<RaptorQReceiver<S>> {
        let config = self.config.unwrap_or_default();
        let source = self.source.ok_or_else(|| {
            Error::new(ErrorKind::InvalidEncodingParams)
                .with_context("source is required")
        })?;

        Ok(RaptorQReceiver {
            config,
            source,
            security_context: self.security_context,
            metrics: self.metrics,
            diagnostic_context: self.diagnostic_context,
        })
    }
}
```

### Pipeline Types

```rust
//! End-to-end RaptorQ pipelines.

use crate::types::symbol::{ObjectId, ObjectParams, Symbol, SymbolId};
use crate::error::{Error, ErrorKind, Result};
use crate::Cx;

/// Trait for symbol sinks (destinations for encoded symbols).
pub trait SymbolSink {
    /// Sends a symbol to the destination.
    fn send(&mut self, symbol: Symbol) -> impl Future<Output = Result<()>> + Send;

    /// Flushes any buffered symbols.
    fn flush(&mut self) -> impl Future<Output = Result<()>> + Send;

    /// Closes the sink, signaling no more symbols will be sent.
    fn close(&mut self) -> impl Future<Output = Result<()>> + Send;
}

/// Trait for symbol sources (origins of received symbols).
pub trait SymbolSource {
    /// Receives the next symbol, if available.
    fn recv(&mut self) -> impl Future<Output = Result<Option<Symbol>>> + Send;

    /// Returns true if the source has been closed.
    fn is_closed(&self) -> bool;
}

/// Sender pipeline for encoding and transmitting objects.
pub struct RaptorQSender<T> {
    config: RaptorQConfig,
    transport: T,
    security_context: Option<SecurityContext>,
    metrics: Option<Metrics>,
    diagnostic_context: Option<DiagnosticContext>,
}

impl<T: SymbolSink> RaptorQSender<T> {
    /// Sends an object by encoding it into symbols and transmitting.
    ///
    /// # Arguments
    ///
    /// * `cx` - Capability context for cancellation and budgets
    /// * `object_id` - Unique identifier for the object
    /// * `data` - Raw bytes to encode and send
    ///
    /// # Returns
    ///
    /// The object parameters used for encoding (needed by receiver).
    pub async fn send_object(
        &mut self,
        cx: &mut Cx<'_>,
        object_id: ObjectId,
        data: &[u8],
    ) -> Result<ObjectParams> {
        // Start tracing span
        let span_id = self.start_span("send_object", object_id);

        // Validate data size
        self.validate_data_size(data)?;

        // Compute encoding parameters
        let params = self.compute_params(object_id, data.len() as u64);

        // Encode into symbols
        let symbols = self.encode(cx, &params, data).await?;

        // Sign symbols if security is enabled
        let symbols = self.sign_symbols(symbols)?;

        // Transmit symbols
        for symbol in symbols {
            cx.checkpoint()?;
            self.transport.send(symbol).await?;
            self.record_symbol_sent();
        }

        self.transport.flush().await?;

        // End tracing span
        self.end_span_ok(span_id);

        Ok(params)
    }

    /// Sends symbols for an already-encoded object.
    pub async fn send_symbols(
        &mut self,
        cx: &mut Cx<'_>,
        symbols: impl IntoIterator<Item = Symbol>,
    ) -> Result<usize> {
        let mut count = 0;
        for symbol in symbols {
            cx.checkpoint()?;

            let signed = self.sign_symbol(symbol)?;
            self.transport.send(signed).await?;
            count += 1;

            self.record_symbol_sent();
        }
        self.transport.flush().await?;
        Ok(count)
    }

    fn validate_data_size(&self, data: &[u8]) -> Result<()> {
        let max_size = (self.config.encoding.max_symbols_per_block as u64)
            * (self.config.encoding.symbol_size as u64)
            * 255; // max source blocks

        if data.len() as u64 > max_size {
            return Err(Error::data_too_large(data.len() as u64, max_size));
        }
        Ok(())
    }

    fn compute_params(&self, object_id: ObjectId, size: u64) -> ObjectParams {
        let symbol_size = self.config.encoding.symbol_size;
        let symbols_per_block = self.config.encoding.max_symbols_per_block;

        let total_symbols = (size / symbol_size as u64) + 1;
        let source_blocks = ((total_symbols / symbols_per_block as u64) + 1).min(255) as u8;

        ObjectParams::new(
            object_id,
            size,
            symbol_size,
            source_blocks,
            symbols_per_block,
        )
    }

    async fn encode(
        &self,
        cx: &mut Cx<'_>,
        params: &ObjectParams,
        data: &[u8],
    ) -> Result<Vec<Symbol>> {
        // Placeholder: actual encoding uses RaptorQ codec
        cx.checkpoint()?;
        let _ = (params, data);
        Ok(Vec::new())
    }

    fn sign_symbols(&self, symbols: Vec<Symbol>) -> Result<Vec<Symbol>> {
        match &self.security_context {
            Some(ctx) if ctx.is_signing_enabled() => {
                symbols.into_iter().map(|s| self.sign_symbol(s)).collect()
            }
            _ => Ok(symbols),
        }
    }

    fn sign_symbol(&self, symbol: Symbol) -> Result<Symbol> {
        // Placeholder: actual signing uses security context
        Ok(symbol)
    }

    fn start_span(&mut self, name: &str, object_id: ObjectId) -> Option<SpanId> {
        self.diagnostic_context.as_mut().map(|ctx| {
            let id = ctx.start_span(name, Time::ZERO);
            ctx.add_attribute("object_id", object_id.to_string());
            id
        })
    }

    fn end_span_ok(&mut self, span_id: Option<SpanId>) {
        if let (Some(ctx), Some(_)) = (&mut self.diagnostic_context, span_id) {
            ctx.end_span_ok(Time::ZERO);
        }
    }

    fn record_symbol_sent(&mut self) {
        if let Some(metrics) = &mut self.metrics {
            metrics.counter("symbols_sent").increment(1);
        }
    }
}

/// Receiver pipeline for receiving and decoding objects.
pub struct RaptorQReceiver<S> {
    config: RaptorQConfig,
    source: S,
    security_context: Option<SecurityContext>,
    metrics: Option<Metrics>,
    diagnostic_context: Option<DiagnosticContext>,
}

impl<S: SymbolSource> RaptorQReceiver<S> {
    /// Receives and decodes an object.
    ///
    /// Blocks until sufficient symbols are received to decode the object,
    /// or the timeout/budget is exhausted.
    pub async fn receive_object(
        &mut self,
        cx: &mut Cx<'_>,
        params: &ObjectParams,
    ) -> Result<Vec<u8>> {
        let span_id = self.start_span("receive_object", params.object_id);

        let mut symbols = Vec::new();
        let needed = params.min_symbols_for_decode();

        // Collect symbols until we have enough
        while symbols.len() < needed as usize {
            cx.checkpoint()?;

            match self.source.recv().await? {
                Some(symbol) => {
                    // Verify if security is enabled
                    let symbol = self.verify_symbol(symbol)?;

                    // Check object match
                    if symbol.object_id() != params.object_id {
                        continue; // Skip symbols for other objects
                    }

                    symbols.push(symbol);
                    self.record_symbol_received();
                }
                None => {
                    return Err(Error::insufficient_symbols(
                        symbols.len() as u32,
                        needed,
                    ));
                }
            }
        }

        // Decode
        let data = self.decode(cx, params, &symbols).await?;

        self.end_span_ok(span_id);
        Ok(data)
    }

    fn verify_symbol(&self, symbol: Symbol) -> Result<Symbol> {
        match &self.security_context {
            Some(ctx) if self.config.security.reject_unauthenticated => {
                // Placeholder: actual verification
                let _ = ctx;
                Ok(symbol)
            }
            _ => Ok(symbol),
        }
    }

    async fn decode(
        &self,
        cx: &mut Cx<'_>,
        params: &ObjectParams,
        symbols: &[Symbol],
    ) -> Result<Vec<u8>> {
        cx.checkpoint()?;
        // Placeholder: actual decoding uses RaptorQ codec
        let _ = (params, symbols);
        Ok(Vec::new())
    }

    fn start_span(&mut self, name: &str, object_id: ObjectId) -> Option<SpanId> {
        self.diagnostic_context.as_mut().map(|ctx| {
            let id = ctx.start_span(name, Time::ZERO);
            ctx.add_attribute("object_id", object_id.to_string());
            id
        })
    }

    fn end_span_ok(&mut self, span_id: Option<SpanId>) {
        if let (Some(ctx), Some(_)) = (&mut self.diagnostic_context, span_id) {
            ctx.end_span_ok(Time::ZERO);
        }
    }

    fn record_symbol_received(&mut self) {
        if let Some(metrics) = &mut self.metrics {
            metrics.counter("symbols_received").increment(1);
        }
    }
}
```

### Preset Configurations

```rust
//! Preset configurations for common use cases.

impl RaptorQConfig {
    /// Configuration optimized for LAN environments.
    ///
    /// - Larger symbols for better throughput
    /// - Minimal overhead
    /// - Fast timeouts
    #[must_use]
    pub fn lan() -> Self {
        Self {
            encoding: EncodingConfig {
                symbol_size: 1400,
                repair_overhead: 0.05,
                ..Default::default()
            },
            decoding: DecodingConfig {
                decode_timeout: Duration::from_secs(5),
                ..Default::default()
            },
            transport: TransportConfig {
                rate_limit: 0,
                idle_timeout: Duration::from_secs(30),
                ..Default::default()
            },
            ..Default::default()
        }
    }

    /// Configuration optimized for WAN/Internet environments.
    ///
    /// - Smaller symbols for MTU compatibility
    /// - Higher overhead for loss tolerance
    /// - Longer timeouts
    #[must_use]
    pub fn wan() -> Self {
        Self {
            encoding: EncodingConfig {
                symbol_size: 1280,
                repair_overhead: 0.2,
                ..Default::default()
            },
            decoding: DecodingConfig {
                decode_timeout: Duration::from_secs(60),
                ..Default::default()
            },
            transport: TransportConfig {
                retry_policy: RetryPolicy {
                    max_attempts: 5,
                    initial_delay: Duration::from_millis(500),
                    max_delay: Duration::from_secs(30),
                    multiplier: 2.0,
                },
                ..Default::default()
            },
            ..Default::default()
        }
    }

    /// Configuration optimized for satellite/high-latency links.
    ///
    /// - Maximum redundancy
    /// - Very long timeouts
    /// - Aggressive retries
    #[must_use]
    pub fn satellite() -> Self {
        Self {
            encoding: EncodingConfig {
                symbol_size: 512,
                repair_overhead: 0.5,
                ..Default::default()
            },
            decoding: DecodingConfig {
                decode_timeout: Duration::from_secs(300),
                decode_threshold: 1.0,
                ..Default::default()
            },
            transport: TransportConfig {
                max_in_flight: 500,
                retry_policy: RetryPolicy {
                    max_attempts: 10,
                    initial_delay: Duration::from_secs(2),
                    max_delay: Duration::from_secs(120),
                    multiplier: 1.5,
                },
                ..Default::default()
            },
            ..Default::default()
        }
    }

    /// Configuration for testing with minimal settings.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn testing() -> Self {
        Self {
            encoding: EncodingConfig {
                symbol_size: 64,
                max_symbols_per_block: 16,
                repair_overhead: 0.0,
                ..Default::default()
            },
            decoding: DecodingConfig {
                decode_timeout: Duration::from_millis(100),
                ..Default::default()
            },
            transport: TransportConfig {
                max_in_flight: 10,
                ..Default::default()
            },
            observability: ObservabilityConfig::testing(),
            ..Default::default()
        }
    }
}

## API Surface

### Public Exports

```rust
// src/raptorq/mod.rs

pub mod config;
pub mod builder;
pub mod pipeline;
pub mod presets;

// Re-exports for convenience
pub use config::{
    RaptorQConfig, EncodingConfig, DecodingConfig, TransportConfig,
    SecurityConfig, BudgetConfig, RetryPolicy,
};
pub use builder::{RaptorQSenderBuilder, RaptorQReceiverBuilder};
pub use pipeline::{RaptorQSender, RaptorQReceiver, SymbolSink, SymbolSource};
```

### Extension Points

```rust
/// Trait for custom encoding strategies.
pub trait EncodingStrategy: Send + Sync {
    /// Encodes data into symbols.
    fn encode(
        &self,
        params: &ObjectParams,
        data: &[u8],
    ) -> impl Future<Output = Result<Vec<Symbol>>> + Send;

    /// Generates additional repair symbols.
    fn generate_repair(
        &self,
        params: &ObjectParams,
        source_symbols: &[Symbol],
        count: usize,
    ) -> impl Future<Output = Result<Vec<Symbol>>> + Send;
}

/// Trait for custom decoding strategies.
pub trait DecodingStrategy: Send + Sync {
    /// Attempts to decode from available symbols.
    fn decode(
        &self,
        params: &ObjectParams,
        symbols: &[Symbol],
    ) -> impl Future<Output = Result<Option<Vec<u8>>>> + Send;

    /// Returns true if decoding is likely to succeed.
    fn can_decode(&self, params: &ObjectParams, symbol_count: usize) -> bool;
}
```

## Integration Patterns

### Basic Usage

```rust
use asupersync::raptorq::{RaptorQConfig, RaptorQSenderBuilder, RaptorQReceiverBuilder};

// Sender side
let config = RaptorQConfig::wan();
let mut sender = RaptorQSenderBuilder::new()
    .with_config(config.clone())
    .with_transport(network_sink)
    .build()?;

let params = sender.send_object(&mut cx, object_id, &data).await?;

// Receiver side (needs params out-of-band or in first symbol)
let mut receiver = RaptorQReceiverBuilder::new()
    .with_config(config)
    .with_source(network_source)
    .build()?;

let data = receiver.receive_object(&mut cx, &params).await?;
```

### With Security

```rust
use asupersync::security::{AuthKey, SecurityContext, AuthMode};

let key = AuthKey::from_seed(42);
let security = SecurityContext::new(key).with_mode(AuthMode::Required);

let sender = RaptorQSenderBuilder::new()
    .with_config(RaptorQConfig::default())
    .with_transport(sink)
    .with_security_context(security.clone())
    .build()?;
```

### With Full Observability

```rust
use asupersync::observability::{
    ObservabilityConfig, Metrics, DiagnosticContext, LogCollector,
};

let obs_config = ObservabilityConfig::development();
let metrics = obs_config.create_metrics().unwrap();
let diag_ctx = obs_config.create_diagnostic_context();

let sender = RaptorQSenderBuilder::new()
    .with_config(RaptorQConfig::default())
    .with_transport(sink)
    .with_metrics(metrics)
    .with_diagnostic_context(diag_ctx)
    .build()?;
```

## Unit Test Scenarios

### Test List (15 tests)

1. **test_config_defaults_are_valid** - Default configuration passes validation
2. **test_config_presets_compile** - All preset configurations (LAN, WAN, satellite) are valid
3. **test_sender_builder_requires_transport** - Builder fails without transport
4. **test_receiver_builder_requires_source** - Builder fails without source
5. **test_builder_type_safety** - Builder pattern enforces required components at compile time
6. **test_send_object_encodes_and_transmits** - End-to-end send with mock transport
7. **test_receive_object_collects_and_decodes** - End-to-end receive with mock source
8. **test_sender_respects_cancellation** - Send aborts on cancellation
9. **test_receiver_timeout_on_insufficient_symbols** - Receiver times out correctly
10. **test_security_context_signs_symbols** - Symbols are signed when security enabled
11. **test_security_context_verifies_symbols** - Invalid signatures are rejected
12. **test_metrics_track_symbol_counts** - Metrics increment on send/receive
13. **test_diagnostic_spans_created** - Spans created for send/receive operations
14. **test_config_validation_rejects_invalid** - Invalid configurations are rejected
15. **test_retry_policy_applies_to_failures** - Retries occur on transient failures

### Example Test Implementation

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    struct MockSink {
        symbols: Vec<Symbol>,
    }

    impl SymbolSink for MockSink {
        async fn send(&mut self, symbol: Symbol) -> Result<()> {
            self.symbols.push(symbol);
            Ok(())
        }
        async fn flush(&mut self) -> Result<()> { Ok(()) }
        async fn close(&mut self) -> Result<()> { Ok(()) }
    }

    struct MockSource {
        symbols: Vec<Symbol>,
        index: usize,
    }

    impl SymbolSource for MockSource {
        async fn recv(&mut self) -> Result<Option<Symbol>> {
            if self.index < self.symbols.len() {
                let s = self.symbols[self.index].clone();
                self.index += 1;
                Ok(Some(s))
            } else {
                Ok(None)
            }
        }
        fn is_closed(&self) -> bool { self.index >= self.symbols.len() }
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_config_defaults_are_valid() {
        let config = RaptorQConfig::default();

        assert!(config.encoding.symbol_size > 0);
        assert!(config.encoding.max_symbols_per_block > 0);
        assert!(config.encoding.repair_overhead >= 0.0);
        assert!(config.decoding.decode_threshold > 0.0);
        assert!(config.decoding.decode_timeout.as_millis() > 0);
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_config_presets_compile() {
        let _ = RaptorQConfig::lan();
        let _ = RaptorQConfig::wan();
        let _ = RaptorQConfig::satellite();
        let _ = RaptorQConfig::testing();
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_sender_builder_requires_transport() {
        let result = RaptorQSenderBuilder::<MockSink>::new()
            .with_config(RaptorQConfig::default())
            .build();

        assert!(result.is_err());
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_builder_with_all_components() {
        let sink = MockSink { symbols: Vec::new() };
        let result = RaptorQSenderBuilder::new()
            .with_config(RaptorQConfig::default())
            .with_transport(sink)
            .build();

        assert!(result.is_ok());
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_metrics_track_symbol_counts() {
        let mut metrics = Metrics::new();
        let sink = MockSink { symbols: Vec::new() };

        let mut sender = RaptorQSenderBuilder::new()
            .with_config(RaptorQConfig::testing())
            .with_transport(sink)
            .with_metrics(metrics.clone())
            .build()
            .unwrap();

        // After sending, counter should increment
        // (implementation detail - verify in integration tests)
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_encoding_config_validation() {
        let mut config = EncodingConfig::default();

        // Zero symbol size should be invalid
        config.symbol_size = 0;
        // Validation would reject this
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_retry_policy_defaults() {
        let policy = RetryPolicy::default();

        assert!(policy.max_attempts >= 1);
        assert!(policy.initial_delay.as_millis() > 0);
        assert!(policy.max_delay >= policy.initial_delay);
        assert!(policy.multiplier >= 1.0);
    }
}
```

## Logging Strategy

### Log Points

| Location | Level | Message | Fields |
|----------|-------|---------|--------|
| `send_object` start | DEBUG | "Starting object send" | `object_id`, `data_len`, `symbol_size` |
| `send_object` encoded | DEBUG | "Encoded object into symbols" | `object_id`, `symbol_count`, `repair_count` |
| `send_object` complete | INFO | "Object sent successfully" | `object_id`, `duration_ms`, `symbols_sent` |
| `send_object` error | WARN | "Failed to send object" | `object_id`, `error`, `symbols_sent` |
| `receive_object` start | DEBUG | "Starting object receive" | `object_id`, `expected_symbols` |
| `receive_object` progress | TRACE | "Received symbol" | `symbol_id`, `progress` |
| `receive_object` complete | INFO | "Object received successfully" | `object_id`, `duration_ms`, `symbols_used` |
| `receive_object` timeout | WARN | "Timed out waiting for symbols" | `object_id`, `received`, `needed` |
| Security violation | WARN | "Rejected unauthenticated symbol" | `symbol_id`, `reason` |
| Config validation | ERROR | "Invalid configuration" | `field`, `value`, `constraint` |

### Structured Log Format

```rust
use crate::observability::{LogEntry, LogLevel};

impl<T: SymbolSink> RaptorQSender<T> {
    fn log_send_start(&self, object_id: ObjectId, data_len: usize) -> LogEntry {
        LogEntry::new(LogLevel::Debug, "Starting object send")
            .with_field("object_id", object_id.to_string())
            .with_field("data_len", data_len.to_string())
            .with_field("symbol_size", self.config.encoding.symbol_size.to_string())
    }

    fn log_send_complete(
        &self,
        object_id: ObjectId,
        duration_ms: u64,
        symbols_sent: usize,
    ) -> LogEntry {
        LogEntry::new(LogLevel::Info, "Object sent successfully")
            .with_field("object_id", object_id.to_string())
            .with_field("duration_ms", duration_ms.to_string())
            .with_field("symbols_sent", symbols_sent.to_string())
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria Checklist

- [ ] **Configuration Facade**
  - [ ] `RaptorQConfig` aggregates all subsystem configs
  - [ ] Default values are sensible and documented
  - [ ] Configuration is validated on construction
  - [ ] Preset configurations (LAN, WAN, satellite, testing) are provided

- [ ] **Builder Patterns**
  - [ ] `RaptorQSenderBuilder` constructs sender pipeline
  - [ ] `RaptorQReceiverBuilder` constructs receiver pipeline
  - [ ] Builders enforce required components
  - [ ] Builders support optional components (security, metrics, diagnostics)

- [ ] **End-to-End Pipelines**
  - [ ] `RaptorQSender::send_object` encodes and transmits
  - [ ] `RaptorQReceiver::receive_object` receives and decodes
  - [ ] Pipelines respect cancellation via `Cx`
  - [ ] Pipelines integrate with security context
  - [ ] Pipelines emit metrics and diagnostic spans

- [ ] **Error Handling**
  - [ ] Invalid configurations produce clear errors
  - [ ] Transport failures are propagated with context
  - [ ] Timeout conditions are handled gracefully
  - [ ] Security violations are logged and rejected

- [ ] **Testing**
  - [ ] All 15+ unit tests pass
  - [ ] Integration tests with mock transport
  - [ ] Tests cover error paths and edge cases
  - [ ] Tests verify metric and span emission

- [ ] **Documentation**
  - [ ] Module-level documentation with examples
  - [ ] All public types have doc comments
  - [ ] Configuration options are documented
  - [ ] Common usage patterns are shown

- [ ] **Code Quality**
  - [ ] `cargo fmt` passes
  - [ ] `cargo clippy` passes (pedantic + nursery)
  - [ ] No `unsafe` code
  - [ ] No panics in public API","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T08:40:46.764680953Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-29T07:46:09.676396201Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T07:46:09.676170592Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3u7","depends_on_id":"bd-00e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:45Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3u7","depends_on_id":"bd-6bp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:45Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3u7","depends_on_id":"bd-b3d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:45Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3u7","depends_on_id":"bd-fke","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:45Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3u7","depends_on_id":"bd-iu1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:45Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3u7","depends_on_id":"bd-o78","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:45Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3u7","depends_on_id":"bd-t3v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:45Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3u7","depends_on_id":"bd-uls","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:45Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3u7","depends_on_id":"bd-ups","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:45Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3u7","depends_on_id":"bd-xtx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:45Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3uag","title":"[AA][Cap] Ambient authority audit + hardening","description":"## Goal
Audit the entire codebase for ambient authority (implicit IO/time/random/spawn).

## Why
Hidden effects break determinism and security. Every effect must flow through explicit capabilities.

## Deliverables
- Audit checklist + grep patterns.
- Fixes for any ambient effects.
- Regression tests to prevent reintroduction.

## Acceptance
- No ambient authority remains in core runtime.
- New violations are caught by CI.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:09:29.456817845Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:59:44.582959700Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T03:59:44.582885202Z","close_reason":"Audit complete in src/audit/ambient.rs with KNOWN_FINDINGS array + CI grep patterns. Found 8 non-exempt violations (3 time, 4 spawn, 2 entropy) and 5 exempt (by design). Added 2 missing spawn violations (server/connection.rs, http/h1/stream.rs). All effects in core runtime flow through Cx capabilities. Regression prevention via GREP_PATTERNS const for CI.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3uag","depends_on_id":"bd-1rfe","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:34Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3uag","depends_on_id":"bd-3uzb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:34Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3ufep","title":"[audit] Fix clippy -D warnings surfaced in unix datagram + scheduler + reactor registration","description":"Fresh-eyes audit slice: fix clippy -D warnings currently failing build:\n- unix datagram Errno::EWOULDBLOCK unreachable patterns (alias of EAGAIN)\n- unix datagram io::Error::new(Other, ...) lint prefers io::Error::other\n- reactor Registration::deregister no-leak implementation + clippy unused_mut\n- scheduler three_lane items_after_statements guard type placement\n- race macro placeholder test compile fix\n\nAcceptance:\n- cargo fmt --check\n- cargo check --all-targets\n- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings\n- cargo test\nAll green.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MaroonMarsh","created_at":"2026-02-09T16:07:33.094163896Z","created_by":"MaroonMarsh","updated_at":"2026-02-09T17:05:28.548777965Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T17:05:28.548709147Z","close_reason":"Completed (gates green; landed in b655d82)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","clippy","hygiene"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ufep","depends_on_id":"bd-2wds9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:43Z","created_by":"import"}]}
